title: `buildah version` errors out
body:
```
# buildah version
strconv.ParseInt: parsing "": invalid syntax
# rpm -q buildah
buildah-1.4-2.dev.git19e44f0.fc30.x86_64
# buildah --debug version
ERRO[0000] strconv.ParseInt: parsing "": invalid syntax 
```

Same error when invoked both as root and as a normal user.

This is Fedora Rawhide.

comments.body:
Interesting, not happening for me on F29, but will take a look.

Thanks for the report @TomasTomecek

@lsm5 the version.go file looks for main.buildInfo variable to get the build time of the Buildah project.  The Makefile code looks like:

```
/Makefile:BUILD_INFO := $(if $(shell date +%s),$(shell date +%s),$(error "date failed"))
./Makefile:LDFLAGS := -ldflags '-X main.gitCommit=${GIT_COMMIT} -X main.buildInfo=${BUILD_INFO} -X main.cniVersion=${CNI_COMMIT}'
```
This seems to be working in Fedora 28/29 and other places just fine, but not apparently in Fedora Rawhide?  Any thoughts as to why?
I have the same problem with the buildah package installed from Fedora 29: `buildah-1.4-3.git608fa84.fc29.x86_64`

Could you try

dnf -y update buildah --enablerepo=updates-testing

And see if it works correctly in buildah 1.5?
This should be fixed in upstream and v1.5 of Buildah.  We just cut the v1.5 on Thursday and are working on getting RPM's together now for it.

> Could you try
> 
> dnf -y update buildah --enablerepo=updates-testing
> 
> And see if it works correctly in buildah 1.5?

I confirm that it works correctly with buildah 1.5, thanks!
